$comment: Reference case for schlick_geometry (G1 = NdotV / (NdotV*(1-k) + k)), the Schlick-GGX geometry term. Single coefficient k. k appears in the denominator on both a constant and an NdotV-scaled term, which makes a cold-start LM search ill-conditioned (it diverges with 'lambda exceeded maximum'). So — like aerodynamic_drag — the canonical value equals the library default: the fit starts on the answer and the test asserts it holds the knee across the sweep.
notes: 21-point sweep over the view cosine NdotV ∈ [0,1]. G1 rises from 0 (at NdotV=0) toward 1 (at NdotV=1); k controls the knee. Canonical k=0.125 matches the library default (the direct-lighting Smith term at roughness 0.5). 5 % relative tolerance because k sits in the denominator and is less sensitive than a pure scale coefficient.
